{cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n45\t    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n46\t    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n47\t    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n48\t\n49\t    Automatic cleanup:\n50\t    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n51\t    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n52\t\n53\t    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n54\t    \"\"\"\n55\t\n56\t    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int every N operations\n70\t\n71\t    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n72\t        \"\"\"\n73\t        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n74\t\n75\t        Args:\n76\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n77\t\n78\t        Returns:\n79\t            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n80\t        \"\"\"\n81\t        # Automatic cleanup: if cache size > 1000, remove all expired entries first\n82\t        # This prevents memory leaks from accumulating expired entries\n83\t        if len(self._cache) > 1000:\n84\t            removed = self._cache:\n89\t            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n90\t\n91\t            # Check if entry has expired\n92\t            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n93\t                self._cache_hits += 1\n94\t                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n95\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n96\t                return intent_mapping\n97\t            else:\n98\t                # Remove expired entry\n99\t                del self._cache[key]\n100\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n101\t\n102\t        # Cache miss or expired\n103\t        self._cache_misses += 1\n104\t        return None\n105\t\n106\t    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n107\t        \"\"\"\n108\t        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n109\t\n110\t        Args:\n111\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n112\t            value: Intent mapping to cache (list[IntentClassification])\n113\t        \"\"\"\n114\t        # Prune cache if at capacity (evict oldest entry - first in dict)\n115\t        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:\n116\t            oldest_key = next(iter(self._cache))\n117\t            del self._cache[oldest_key]\n118\t            logger.debug(f\"Cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n119\t\n120\t        # {key[:8]} (cache size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n124\t\n125\t    def _cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n126\t        \"\"\"\n127\t        Remove all expired entries from the cache.\n128\t\n129\t        Returns:\n130\t            Number of entries removed\n131\t        \"\"\"\n132\t        current_time = time.time()\n133\t        expired_keys = [\n134\t            key for key, (_, expiry) in self._cache.items()\n135\t            if expiry < current_time\n136\t        ]\n137\t\n138\t        for key in expired_keys:\n139\t            del self._cache[key]\n140\t\n141\t        if dict[str, Any]:\n147\t        \"\"\"\n148\t        Get cache statistics.\n149\t\n150\t        Returns:\n151\t            Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, size\n152\t        \"\"\"\n153\t        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n154\t        hit_rate = (self._cache_hits / total_requests * 100) if total_requests > 0 else 0.0\n155\t\n156\t        return {\n157\t            \"hits\": self._cache_hits,\n158\t            \"misses\": self._cache_misses,\n159\t            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n160\t            \"size\": len(self._cache),\n161\t        }\n162\t\n163\t    def clear(self) -> None:\n164\t        \"\"\"Clear the cache and reset statistics.\"\"\"\n165\t        self._cache.clear()\n166\t        self._cache_hits = 0\n167\t        self._cache_misses = interval.\n173\t\n174\t        Returns:\n175\t            True if stats should be logged, False otherwise\n176\t        \"\"\"\n177\t        total_requests = self._cache_hits + Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n252\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n253\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n254\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n255\t\n256\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n257\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n258\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n259\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n260\t    \"intent objects.\"\n261\t)\n262\t\n263\t\n264\tclass IntentRouter:\n265\t    \"\"\"\n266\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n267\t\n268\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n269\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n270\t\n271\t    Latency optimizations (adc-1kp7n):\n272\t    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n273\t    - Simplified prompt (removed confidence field/redundant rules)\n274\t    - Reduced max_tokens (80 vs 96) for faster generation\n275\t    - Temperature 0.0 for deterministic sampling\n276\t    - Dedicated 8s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n277\t    \"\"\"\n278\t\n279\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None, cache_ttl: int = 300):\n280\t        self.store = store\n281\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n282\t        self._zai_client = None\n283\t        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n284\t        self._reload_manager = None\n285\t        self._cache = IntentCache(ttl_seconds=cache_ttl)\n286\t\n287\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n288\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n289\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n290\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n291\t        return self._zai_client\n292\t\n293\t    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n294\t        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n295\t        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n296\t            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n297\t            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n298\t            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n299\t            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n300\t        return self._router_zai_client\n301\t\n302\t    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n303\t        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n304\t        import hashlib\n305\t        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n306\t\n307\t    def generate_cache_key(self, utterance: str, intent_type_context: str | None = None) -> str:\n308\t        \"\"\"\n309\t        Generate a SHA256 hash key for caching based on utterance and optional context.\n310\t\n311\t        Args:\n312\t            utterance: The user utterance to hash\n313\t            intent_type_context: Optional context string (e.g., intent type, session state)\n314\t\n315\t        Returns:\n316\t            SHA256 hex digest (32 characters) of the combined utterance and context\n317\t\n318\t        Examples:\n319\t            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", \"status\")\n320\t            'a1b2c3d4e5f6...'  # 32-character hex string\n321\t            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", None)  # Same as no context\n322\t            'differenthashvalue...'  # Different hash without context\n323\t        \"\"\"\n324\t        import hashlib\n325\t\n326\t        # Handle None/empty context gracefully - treat as empty string\n327\t        context = intent_type_context or \"\"\n328\t\n329\t        # Combine utterance and context with a delimiter to avoid collisions\n330\t        # e.g., \"test\" + \"status\" vs \"teststatus\" + \"\"\n331\t        combined = f\"{utterance}|{context}\"\n332\t\n333\t        # Generate SHA256 hash and return hex digest (32 characters)\n334\t        return hashlib.sha256(combined.encode()).hexdigest()\n335\t\n336\t    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n337\t        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n338\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n339\t        return self._cache.get(cache_key)\n340\t\n341\t    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n342\t        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n343\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n344\t        self._cache.set(cache_key, classifications)\n345\t\n346\t    def _get_cache_stats_string(self) -> str:\n347\t        \"\"\"\n348\t        Get formatted cache statistics string for logging.\n349\t\n350\t        Returns:\n351\t            Formatted string with cache hit rate and entries count\n352\t        \"\"\"\n353\t        stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n354\t        return f\"cache_hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.2f}% entries={stats['size']}\"\n355\t\n356\t    def _log_cache_stats_if_needed(self) -> None:\n357\t        \"\"\"Log cache hit rate statistics periodically.\"\"\"\n358\t        if self._cache.should_log_stats():\n359\t            stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n360\t            logger.info(\n361\t                f\"Router cache statistics: \"\n362\t                f\"hits={stats['hits']} misses={stats['misses']} \"\n363\t                f\"hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.1f}% \"\n364\t                f\"total_requests={stats['hits'] + stats['misses']} \"\n365\t                f\"cache_size={stats['size']}\"\n366\t            )\n367\t\n368\t    def _clear_cache(self) -> None:\n369\t        \"\"\"Clear the router cache. Used primarily for testing and diagnostics.\"\"\"\n370\t        self._cache.clear()\n371\t\n372\t    async def _get_store(self):\n373\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n374\t        if self.store is None:\n375\t            self.store = get_store()\n376\t        return self.store\n377\t\n378\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n379\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n380\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n381\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n382\t        return self._reload_manager\n383\t\n384\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n385\t        \"\"\"\n386\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n387\t\n388\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n389\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n390\t        \"\"\"\n391\t        try:\n392\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n393\t        except Exception as e:\n394\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n395\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n396\t\n397\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n398\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n399\t\n400\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n401\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n402\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n403\t        \"\"\"\n404\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n405\t\n406\t    async def classify_utterance(\n407\t        self,\n408\t        utterance: str,\n409\t        session_id: str,\n410\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n411\t    ) -> tuple[list[IntentClassification], dict]:\n412\t        \"\"\"\n413\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n414\t\n415\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n416\t\n417\t        Args:\n418\t            utterance: The user utterance\n419\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n420\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n421\t\n422\t        Returns:\n423\t            Tuple of (classifications list, timing_breakdown dict)\n424\t\n425\t        Raises:\n426\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n427\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n428\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n429\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n430\t        \"\"\"\n431\t        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n432\t        cache_check_start = time.perf_counter()\n433\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n434\t        cache_check_ms = (time.perf_counter() - cache_check_start) * 1000\n435\t\n436\t        if cached_result is not None:\n437\t            # Log cache statistics periodically\n438\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n439\t\n440\t            # Log cache hit with cache statistics in structured timing format\n441\t            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n442\t            logger.debug(\n443\t                f\"router_timing phase=cache_check duration_ms={cache_check_ms:.2f} \"\n444\t                f\"cached=True intents={len(cached_result)} {cache_stats}\"\n445\t            )\n446\t            logger.info(\n447\t                f\"Cache HIT: {len(cached_result)} intents ({cache_check_ms:.0f}ms)\"\n448\t            )\n449\t\n450\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n451\t            empty_breakdown = {\n452\t                \"cached\": True,\n453\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n454\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n455\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n456\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n457\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n458\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n459\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n460\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n461\t            }\n462\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n463\t\n464\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n465\t        retry_count = 0\n466\t        raw_response = None\n467\t\n468\t        try:\n469\t            # Measure total classification time\n470\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n471\t\n472\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n473\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n474\t\n475\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n476\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n477\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n478\t\n479\t            # Build user message\n480\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n481\t\n482\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n483\t\n484\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n485\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n486\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n487\t\n488\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n489\t\n490\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n491\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n492\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n493\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n494\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n495\t                user_message=user_message,\n496\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n497\t                max_tokens=80,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 96 to 80 - intent JSON is typically 40-70 tokens (single: ~40-50, multi: ~60-80)\n498\t                temperature=0.0,\n499\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n500\t            )\n501\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n502\t\n503\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n504\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n505\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n506\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n507\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n508\t\n509\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n510\t            raw_response = response\n511\t\n512\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n513\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n514\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n515\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n516\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n517\t\n518\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n519\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n520\t            try:\n521\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n522\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n523\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n524\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n525\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n526\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n527\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n528\t                raise e\n529\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n530\t\n531\t            # Measure classification processing time\n532\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n533\t            classifications = []\n534\t\n535\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n536\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n537\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n538\t                try:\n539\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n540\t                except ValueError:\n541\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n542\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n543\t\n544\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n545\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n546\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n547\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n548\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n549\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n550\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n551\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n552\t                )\n553\t                classifications.append(classification)\n554\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n555\t\n556\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n557\t\n558\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling at DEBUG level\n559\t            # Structured format: phase=duration_ms for easy parsing\n560\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n561\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n562\t            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n563\t            logger.debug(\n564\t                f\"router_timing phase=prompt_construction duration_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n565\t                f\"phase=proxy_call duration_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n566\t                f\"phase=proxy_network duration_ms={network_str} \"\n567\t                f\"phase=proxy_inference duration_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n568\t                f\"phase=json_parse duration_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n569\t                f\"phase=process duration_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n570\t                f\"phase=total duration_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n571\t                f\"intents_count={len(classifications)} {cache_stats}\"\n572\t            )\n573\t            logger.info(\n574\t                f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance ({total_ms:.0f}ms total)\"\n575\t            )\n576\t\n577\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n578\t\n579\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n580\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n581\t            timing_breakdown = {\n582\t                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n583\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n584\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n585\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n586\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n587\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n588\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n589\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n590\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n591\t            }\n592\t\n593\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n594\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n595\t\n596\t            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n597\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n598\t\n599\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n600\t\n601\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n602\t            \"\"\"\n603\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n604\t\n605\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n606\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n607\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n608\t            \"\"\"\n609\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n610\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n611\t\n612\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n613\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n614\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n615\t                retry_count += 1\n616\t                try:\n617\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n618\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n619\t                        utterance=utterance,\n620\t                        session_id=session_id,\n621\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n622\t                    )\n623\t                    return classifications, _\n624\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n625\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n626\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n627\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n628\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n629\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n630\t                    ) from retry_e\n631\t\n632\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n633\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n634\t                parse_error=str(e),\n635\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n636\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n637\t            ) from e\n638\t\n639\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n640\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n641\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n642\t\n643\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n644\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n645\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n646\t\n647\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n648\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n649\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n650\t\n651\t        except Exception as e:\n652\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n653\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n654\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n655\t\n656\t    async def route_utterance(\n657\t        self,\n658\t        utterance: str,\n659\t        utterance_id: str,\n660\t        session_id: str,\n661\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n662\t        \"\"\"\n663\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n664\t\n665\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n666\t\n667\t        Args:\n668\t            utterance: The user utterance\n669\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n670\t            session_id: Session ID\n671\t\n672\t        Returns:\n673\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n674\t\n675\t        Raises:\n676\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n677\t        \"\"\"\n678\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n679\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n680\t\n681\t        try:\n682\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n683\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n684\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n685\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n686\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n687\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n688\t\n689\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n690\t            try:\n691\t                store = await self._get_store()\n692\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n693\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n694\t            except Exception as e:\n695\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n696\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n697\t\n698\t            routed_intents = []\n699\t            for classification in classifications:\n700\t                # Create intent ID\n701\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n702\t\n703\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n704\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n705\t                    classification=classification,\n706\t                    session_id=session_id,\n707\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n708\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n709\t                )\n710\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n711\t\n712\t            return routed_intents\n713\t\n714\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n715\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n716\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n717\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n718\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n719\t                utterance=utterance,\n720\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n721\t                session_id=session_id,\n722\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n723\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n724\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n725\t            )\n726\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n727\t\n728\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n729\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n730\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n731\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n732\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n733\t                utterance=utterance,\n734\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n735\t                session_id=session_id,\n736\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n737\t            )\n738\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n739\t\n740\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n741\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n742\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n743\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n744\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n745\t                utterance=utterance,\n746\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n747\t                session_id=session_id,\n748\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n749\t            )\n750\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n751\t\n752\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n753\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n754\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n755\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n756\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n757\t                utterance=utterance,\n758\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n759\t                session_id=session_id,\n760\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n761\t            )\n762\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n763\t\n764\t    async def process_intent(\n765\t        self,\n766\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n767\t    ) -> dict:\n768\t        \"\"\"\n769\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n770\t\n771\t        Args:\n772\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n773\t\n774\t        Returns:\n775\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n776\t        \"\"\"\n777\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n778\t\n779\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n780\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n781\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n782\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n783\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n784\t        # the dispatch itself.\n785\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n786\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n787\t\n788\t        # Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n859\t        try:\n860\t            store = await self._get_store()\n861\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n862\t getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --- Intent Cache with TTL ----------------------------------------------------\nclass IntentCache:\n    \"\"\"\n    In-memory cache store for intent classifications with TTL support.\n\n    Storage format: {cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n\n    Automatic cleanup:\n    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n\n    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300, max_size: int = 1000):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the cache.\n\n        Args:\n            ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for cache entries in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)\n            max_size: Maximum number of entries before eviction (default: 1000)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n        self.max_size = max_size\n        self._cache: dict[str, tuple[list, float]] = {}\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        self._stats_log_interval = 50  # Log stats every N operations\n\n    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n\n        Returns:\n            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        # Automatic cleanup: if cache size > 1000, remove all expired entries first\n        # This prevents memory leaks from accumulating expired entries\n        if len(self._cache) > 1000:\n            removed = self._cleanup_expired()\n            if removed > 0:\n                logger.debug(f\"Auto-cleanup removed {removed} expired entries (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        if key in self._cache:\n            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n\n            # Check if entry has expired\n            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n                self._cache_hits += 1\n                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return intent_mapping\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del self._cache[key]\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n\n        # Cache miss or expired\n        self._cache_misses += 1\n        return None\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n            value: Intent mapping to cache (list[IntentClassification])\n        \"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity (evict oldest entry - first in dict)\n        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:\n            oldest_key = next(iter(self._cache))\n            del self._cache[oldest_key]\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        # Store with expiry timestamp\n        expiry_timestamp = time.time() + self.ttl_seconds\n        self._cache[key] = (value, expiry_timestamp)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cache STORED key {key[:8]} (cache size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n    def _cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Remove all expired entries from the cache.\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of entries removed\n        \"\"\"\n        current_time = time.time()\n        expired_keys = [\n            key for key, (_, expiry) in self._cache.items()\n            if expiry < current_time\n        ]\n\n        for key in expired_keys:\n            del self._cache[key]\n\n        if expired_keys:\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache cleanup removed {len(expired_keys)} expired entries\")\n\n        return len(expired_keys)\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get cache statistics.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, size\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        hit_rate = (self._cache_hits / total_requests * 100) if total_requests > 0 else 0.0\n\n        return {\n            \"hits\": self._cache_hits,\n            \"misses\": self._cache_misses,\n            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n            \"size\": len(self._cache),\n        }\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear the cache and reset statistics.\"\"\"\n        self._cache.clear()\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        logger.debug(\"Cache cleared\")\n\n    def should_log_stats(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if stats should be logged based on operation interval.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if stats should be logged, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        return total_requests > 0 and total_requests % self._stats_log_interval == 0\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP
